title: Tanacetum nuttallii
taxonomy: Tanacetum nuttallii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Tanacetum, which comprises approximately 60 to 80 species of perennial or annual herbs. Tanacetum nuttallii is native to the mountainous regions of western North America, specifically in the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The plant's classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf and flower morphology. Tanacetum nuttallii is often associated with rocky outcrops and open woodlands in its native range.
uses: Tanacetum nuttallii is also known as Mountain Tansy or Western Tansy, and it has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and flowers contain compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may be beneficial in treating certain health conditions. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate headaches. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential uses. As with any herbal remedy, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Tanacetum nuttallii for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Tanacetum nuttallii, also known as gray diskflower, is a plant that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, but its side effects are not well documented.
